facts.statement: Palo Alto Networks, Inc. is the company name reflected in SEC submission metadata for ticker PANW, and it trades on Nasdaq.
The company reported FY2025 revenue of $6.893 billion.
The company reported FY2025 net income of $439.7 million.
The company reported FY2025 operating cash flow of $2.7775 billion.
The company reported FY2025 research and development expense of $1.604 billion.
The company reported cash and cash equivalents of $1.1353 billion at FY2025.
The source material shows long-term debt of $3.6768 billion for FY2023.
The company reported stockholders' equity of $210 million for FY2025.
The company states it has one operating and reportable segment.
The company states it conducts business globally and that sales are primarily managed on a geographic theater basis.

The chief operating decision maker is the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, who reviews consolidated financial information together with revenue information for resource allocation and performance evaluation.
The company says its CODM uses consolidated net income as the measure of segment profit or loss.

In its 2025 proxy materials, the company says it is transforming cybersecurity by uniting Network Security, SASE, Cloud Security, Security Operations, and soon Identity Security into an integrated platform.
The company says it launched Prisma AIRS, which it describes as a unified AI security platform intended to provide end-to-end security across AI applications, agents, models, and data sets.
The company reported Next-Generation Security Annual Recurring Revenue of $5.58 billion in fiscal 2025, according to the proxy materials.
The proxy materials say Network Security NGS ARR reached $3.9 billion in fiscal 2025 and increased 35% year over year.
The proxy materials say Security Operations NGS ARR reached $1.7 billion in fiscal 2025 and increased approximately 25% year over year.
The proxy materials say remaining performance obligations reached $15.8 billion in fiscal 2025.
The proxy materials say the Cortex XSIAM customer base increased by more than twofold year over year in fiscal 2025.
The 2025 proxy materials list Nikesh Arora as Chief Executive Officer and Chair of the Board, and Dipak Golechha as Chief Financial Officer.
The proxy materials say Nir Zuk announced his retirement and Lee Klarich now serves as Chief Product and Technology Officer and a member of the Board.

The 2025 proxy materials list a board that includes Aparna Bawa, John M. Donovan, Carl Eschenbach, James J. Goetz, Ralph Hamers, Rt Hon Sir John Key, Mary Pat McCarthy, Helle Thorning-Schmidt, and Lorraine Twohill, along with Nikesh Arora and Lee Klarich.
For fiscal 2025, the proxy materials state that 100% of named executive officers' equity compensation awards were performance-based.
The chair letter says the company engaged with shareholders representing approximately 53% of outstanding shares as of June 30, 2025.

The 2025 proxy materials disclose that Nikesh Arora adopted a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan on June 24, 2025 covering 846,408 shares, with an original expiration date of December 24, 2025 or when all shares have been sold.
